Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This attack targets water treatment plants using publicly known vulnerabilities in SCADA systems and exploiting internal weaknesses to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Active Directory Network Traffic File Application Log
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method Custom Command and Control Protocol Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) File Creation (Mechanism) File Modification (Mechanism) Application Log Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit known vulnerabilities in the water treatment plant's SCADA systems through public-facing interfaces.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attackers use compromised credentials to establish a custom command and control channel for further attacks.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers manipulate water pressure and flow rates to cause disruptions and potential contamination.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers move laterally within the network to reach critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers delete sensitive data related to water quality monitoring and treatment processes.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers elevate privileges to gain access to more sensitive areas of the system.

Scenario.summary: This attack scenario involves exploiting publicly known vulnerabilities in SCADA systems and internal weaknesses to disrupt water treatment plant operations.
